label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what we can infer from the Toy Fair 2014 information given. The options are A through D. First, I need to read the passage carefully. The main points mentioned are about the Toy Fair 2014, which was the biggest in its history with over 1,000 companies. The trends include oversized toys, which the expert says are "really big and really out there". The "Larger Than Life" trend is highlighted as a favorite, emphasizing big toys with big play value. Also, there's a mention of a push towards education with STEAM (science, tech, engineering, art, math), including word games and high-tech games like 3D and robots. But the key here is what we can infer. Looking at the options: A) Toys are becoming more educational than fun. The passage does say there's a push towards education, but it doesn't say they are becoming more educational than fun. It's a balance; they mention both educational aspects and fun elements like high-tech games and oversized toys. So maybe A is too extreme? B) Robots are great favorites of buyers. The passage mentions that robots were seen, and technology is influential, but does it say they are "great favorites"? It says "we saw some super cool, high-tech games... robots" and that tech influences toys. However, being present doesn't necessarily mean they are the favorite. The "Larger Than Life" trend was the favorite according to the text. So B might not be correct. C) Big toys will be popular this year. The passage states that the trend is "oversize toys" and "Larger Than Life" was a favorite, indicating big toys with big play value. The expert also says in 2014 everything is really big. So this seems like a valid inference. C looks correct. D) Simple card games will disappear on the market. The passage mentions that companies showed products from simple card games to high-tech robots. It doesn't say card games
